title: OpenAI Launches $14B Deployment Company to Embed AI in Enterprise Operations
summary: OpenAI has launched the OpenAI Deployment Company, a $14 billion AI consulting arm backed by $4 billion in investment, to help businesses deploy AI systems in production.
content: OpenAI is launching the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new business designed to embed specialized Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) inside organizations to build and deploy production-ready AI systems. The company launches with more than $4 billion in initial investment from 19 founding partners including Goldman Sachs, SoftBank, and McKinsey & Company, giving it a pre-money valuation of $14 billion. Alongside the launch, OpenAI announced the acquisition of Tomoro, an applied AI consulting firm, which will contribute approximately 150 experienced engineers from day one. The company is majority-owned by OpenAI and will work with businesses across complex, mission-critical environments — such as Tesco, Virgin Atlantic, and Supercell — to connect AI models to customers' data, tools, and workflows. The goal is to move organizations from isolated AI experiments to durable, operational systems that improve as new frontier models and deployment patterns become available.

title: EU Reaches Landmark Deal to Amend AI Act, Extending Compliance Deadlines
summary: European co-legislators struck a provisional agreement to reform the AI Act, postponing high-risk AI compliance deadlines and exempting industrial machinery from direct applicability.
content: After marathon overnight negotiations, the European Parliament and Council reached a provisional agreement to amend the AI Act as part of the Digital Omnibus simplification package. The deal replaces the looming August 2, 2026 deadline with two distinct timelines: AI systems in biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, law enforcement, and border management must comply by December 2, 2027, while AI embedded in physical products has until August 2, 2028. Machinery has been exempted from direct AI Act applicability where overlap with the Machinery Regulation exists — a key win for Germany, which pushed hard for fewer industrial constraints. The reform also bans so-called 'nudifier' apps and AI-generated child sexual abuse material starting December 2026, and reduces the grace period for AI-generated content transparency requirements from six months to three. Consumer advocates warned the deal creates dangerous loopholes, while industry groups largely welcomed the extended timelines and reduced administrative burden.

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Realtime-2 and New Voice API Models for Developers
summary: OpenAI launched three new real-time audio models — GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper — bringing GPT-5-class reasoning directly into live voice applications.
content: OpenAI introduced three new audio models in its Realtime API, enabling developers to build voice experiences that reason, translate, and transcribe in real time without a traditional transcription-to-synthesis pipeline. GPT-Realtime-2, the flagship, is the first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning capabilities, handling complex requests and maintaining conversational context natively in the audio loop. GPT-Realtime-Translate supports live speech-to-speech translation across more than 70 input languages and 13 output languages, while GPT-Realtime-Whisper delivers low-latency streaming transcription for applications like live captions and meeting notes. Benchmarks show GPT-Realtime-2 scores 15.2% higher on Big Bench Audio and 13.8% higher on Audio MultiChallenge compared to its predecessor. Early production customers including Zillow, Deutsche Telekom, Priceline, and Intercom are already deploying the new models in voice agent workflows.

title: Trump Administration Establishes Binding Pre-Release AI Model Oversight Framework
summary: The US government shifted from voluntary AI guidelines to a formal oversight system, securing binding agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI for mandatory pre-release security evaluations.
content: The Trump administration announced a major AI policy shift in early May 2026, transitioning from a voluntary framework to a structured, mandatory system for overseeing new frontier AI models. The Center for AI Standards and Innovation announced binding agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI on May 5, granting the federal government early access to evaluate AI systems for security risks before public release and requiring continuous post-deployment assessment. White House officials had met the prior week with executives from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI to brief them on the plans, though those two companies had not yet signed formal agreements as of May 6. The policy shift was partly catalyzed by the so-called 'Mythos crisis,' an incident involving Anthropic's restricted Claude Mythos model, and by the rapid deployment of increasingly capable models like GPT-5.5. A presidential executive order is reportedly under consideration to create a working group of government officials and tech leaders tasked with designing permanent, standardized AI governance rules.

title: ServiceNow Launches Otto and Expanded AI Control Tower at Knowledge 2026
summary: ServiceNow unveiled Otto and sweeping AI Control Tower updates at Knowledge 2026, positioning itself as the governance and execution layer for enterprise agentic AI.
content: At its annual Knowledge 2026 conference in Las Vegas, ServiceNow launched a set of AI platform updates designed to turn enterprise AI investment into measurable operational outcomes. The centerpiece is ServiceNow Otto, a new enterprise AI experience that unifies conversational AI, autonomous workflows, and search into a single interface that executes work end-to-end across any enterprise system. The expanded AI Control Tower now covers discovery across 30+ enterprise integrations, real-time observability of agent behavior, automated risk and compliance controls, and identity governance extended to AI models themselves. ServiceNow also opened its platform to third-party agents built on Claude, Copilot, or custom models via a generally available MCP Server under the new Action Fabric framework. The company cited internal results — over 90% of employee IT requests handled autonomously, cases resolved 99% faster — and set a target of $30 billion-plus in subscription revenues by 2030, with AI expected to represent more than 30% of annual contract value.

title: SAP and Cyberwave Deploy Fully Autonomous AI Robots in Live Logistics Warehouse
summary: SAP and Cyberwave announced the successful deployment of fully autonomous AI-powered robots in a live SAP warehouse in Germany, performing real fulfillment tasks without human intervention.
content: SAP and AI robotics software firm Cyberwave have deployed fully autonomous robots in SAP's active logistics warehouse in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, marking a significant milestone for Physical AI in enterprise operations. The robots handle box folding, packaging, and in-house shipping fulfillment tasks entirely autonomously via SAP's Logistics Management platform and the Cyberwave model training stack. Cyberwave's platform allows non-expert operators to train robots through intuitive demonstrations, then fine-tunes Vision-Language-Action (VLA) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) models on that data — reducing task training time from weeks to hours and enabling robots to generalize across object types and workflow variations rather than following scripted motions. The full integration, from robot training to live operation, was completed via SAP Business Technology Platform and Cyberwave's platform, with tasks translated into precise robot commands through the SAP Embodied AI Service. The deployment demonstrates that Physical AI has crossed from research concept to measurable throughput improvement in active production logistics environments.
